description: Change ready_issues() in db.rs to remove the hardcoded bug exclusion. Replace 'issue_type IN (task, test, chore)' with a condition that includes bugs only when they have zero non-closed fixes children: (issue_type \!= 'bug' OR id NOT IN (SELECT fixes FROM issues WHERE fixes IS NOT NULL AND status \!= 'closed')). title: Update auto-close to all-or-nothing for multi-fix bugs
description: Change close_issue() in db.rs to check whether ALL issues with fixes pointing to the same bug are closed before auto-closing the bug. Currently (db.rs:312-327) the first fix task to close immediately closes the bug. New behavior: query 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE fixes = ?bug_id AND status \!= closed AND id \!= ?closing_task_id'. If count > 0, skip auto-close. If 0, auto-close with reason 'fixed' (not 'fixed by <task-id>' since multiple tasks may contribute). See specs/pensa.md fixes field description. Source: crates/pensa/src/db.rs:312-327.

title: Implement double Ctrl+C confirmation in run_afk
description: In run_afk(), change the interrupt polling loop to implement double-press logic: (1) When sigint_count == 1: print 'Press Ctrl+C again to stop' to stdout, record Instant::now() as confirmation window start. (2) When sigint_count >= 2: kill child, return. (3) On each poll iteration, if confirmation window is active and 2 seconds have elapsed: reset sigint_count to 0 via store(0, Relaxed). Message goes to stdout only (not log file). See specs/ralph.md § Double Ctrl+C in AFK Mode. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 343-471 (run_afk function).

sgf kills ralph on first Ctrl+C in AFK mode — double-press never works. orchestrate.rs registers SIGINT with AtomicBool (single-press semantics), so sgf sends SIGTERM to ralph within 50ms of the first Ctrl+C, before the user can press again. Ralph's double-Ctrl+C mechanism is correct but never gets a chance to execute.
close_reason: fixed

title: Replace AtomicBool SIGINT handler with AtomicUsize counter in orchestrate::run_ralph for AFK mode. Use signal_hook::low_level::register (like ralph does) to increment a counter. While the ralph child is running in AFK mode: first SIGINT does nothing (let ralph handle it), second SIGINT within 2s sends SIGTERM to ralph child + stops sandbox + exits 130, timeout resets counter to 0. SIGTERM always kills immediately. Outside the child-running loop (pre-launch, daemon startup, etc.), single SIGINT exits immediately.

Sandbox 'already exists' message leaks into ralph output
description: ralph's ensure_sandbox() calls `docker sandbox create` without checking if the sandbox already exists. When it does exist, Docker prints 'sandbox with name claude-<workspace> already exists' to stdout, which leaks into ralph's output between the banner and the first iteration. Fix: extract a sandbox_name() helper (shared with configure_sandbox_network), check `docker sandbox ls -q` for the name, and only call `create` if absent. Suppress stdout/stderr on the `create` call. See specs/ralph.md 'Main Loop' and crates/ralph/src/main.rs:111-166.

title: Fix: ralph save/restore terminal settings (tcgetattr/tcsetattr) around agent spawn to prevent agent from disabling ISIG via inherited stderr fd
description: The agent (Claude Code) modifies terminal settings via inherited stderr fd (e.g., tcsetattr to enable raw mode), disabling ISIG. This causes Ctrl+C to send byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to send 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: save terminal settings before agent spawn, restore after exit. Affects both AFK and interactive modes. See specs/ralph.md Terminal Settings Preservation section.
close_reason: Implemented terminal settings save/restore via tcgetattr/tcsetattr around agent spawn in ralph main.rs

title: Fix: sgf gate monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run_ralph
description: orchestrate.rs run_ralph() unconditionally uses setsid() and monitor_stdin: true. In non-AFK (interactive) mode this causes: (1) stdin monitor thread consumes user keystrokes meant for Claude, (2) setsid prevents child from receiving terminal signals and accessing controlling terminal. Fix: only use setsid() and monitor_stdin: true when config.afk is true. See specs/springfield.md and specs/shutdown.md.
close_reason: Gated monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run() and run_ralph(). Added test for non-AFK mode.

title: AFK mode stdin isolation: agent inherits terminal fd and disables ISIG, preventing Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D from working
description: In AFK mode, sgf passes Stdio::inherit() for stdin to ralph, and ralph doesn't set stdin on the agent spawn. The agent (Claude Code) inherits the terminal fd and modifies terminal settings (disabling ISIG via tcsetattr). This causes Ctrl+C to emit byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to emit byte 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: pass Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf→ralph and ralph→agent boundaries.
close_reason: Fixed by passing Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf->ralph and ralph->agent boundaries. Updated specs/shutdown.md, specs/ralph.md, specs/springfield.md to document stdin isolation.

title: Process leak exhausts macOS fork limit during repeated cargo test --workspace runs
description:
## Symptoms

After running cargo test --workspace repeatedly (e.g., 25 iterations in a Ralph build loop), the macOS per-user process limit (ulimit -u = 2666) is exhausted. All fork() calls fail with EAGAIN / WouldBlock (OS error 35) / "Resource temporarily unavailable". The system remains in this state even after the build loop finishes — processes are leaked, not just bursting.

## Root Cause

Rust's std::process::Child has NO Drop implementation. When a Child handle is dropped without calling .wait() or .kill()+.wait(), the child process keeps running (or becomes a zombie). This happens through 4 distinct leak vectors in the codebase:

### Leak Vector 1: Fire-and-forget spawns
- ralph/src/main.rs:433 — afplay spawned in ding_watcher(), Child immediately dropped. Process detaches and runs orphaned.
- pensa/src/main.rs:342 — daemon spawned, Child dropped. Daemon runs forever (by design, but untracked).
- springfield/src/recovery.rs:164 — same daemon pattern.

### Leak Vector 2: Test .spawn() without panic-safe cleanup
- springfield/tests/integration.rs — 13 tests use .spawn() followed by .wait_with_output(), but if any assertion panics before reaching .wait_with_output(), the Child is dropped without reaping. The sgf process (and its descendants) leak.

### Leak Vector 3: setsid() makes children unkillable from parent
- orchestrate.rs, cursus/runner.rs, ralph/main.rs — AFK mode spawns children with pre_exec(setsid()), putting them in their own session. If the parent is killed (e.g., by test cleanup), children in a separate session do NOT receive the signal. They survive indefinitely.
- Combined with Leak Vector 2: ChildGuard kills sgf's process group, but mock ralph (in its own session via setsid) escapes.

### Leak Vector 4: No cross-crate process semaphore in ralph
- ralph/tests/integration.rs has 62 tests with 58 unguarded .output() calls. While .output() blocks and reaps on the happy path, panics during execution leak the child. ralph also lacks the ProcessSemaphore concurrency control that springfield/pensa/claude-wrapper already have.

## Accumulation Math

Each build loop iteration runs cargo test --workspace (~268 tests). Some subset leaks processes. Over 25 iterations: 25 × leaked_per_run exceeds ulimit -u of 2666. The count is monotonically increasing because leaked processes are never cleaned up.

## Fix Plan

1. Add ChildGuard RAII wrapper to shutdown crate — kills process group + reaps zombie on Drop
2. Add ProcessSemaphore to shutdown crate — consolidate 3 identical copies from test files
3. Add SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID env var — skip setsid() in tests so children stay killable
4. Fix 13 springfield test .spawn() sites to use ChildGuard
5. Fix afplay fire-and-forget in ralph
6. Apply ProcessSemaphore to ralph tests

## Reproduction

Run cargo test --workspace in a loop 10+ times. Monitor process count — it grows monotonically. Eventually fork() fails.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add ChildGuard RAII wrapper to shutdown crate
description:
Add a ChildGuard struct to crates/shutdown/src/lib.rs that wraps std::process::Child with kill-on-drop semantics.

## API

- ChildGuard::spawn(cmd: &mut Command) — sets process_group(0), spawns, wraps result
- ChildGuard::new(child: Child) — wraps existing Child (caller manages process group)
- guard.id() -> u32 — returns PID
- guard.child_mut() -> &mut Child — access stdout/stderr handles
- guard.wait_with_output() -> io::Result<Output> — consumes inner Child, prevents kill-on-drop
- guard.try_wait() -> io::Result<Option<ExitStatus>> — non-consuming check

## Drop Behavior

1. If child consumed by wait_with_output(): no-op
2. Try kill_process_group(pid, timeout) — works if child is process group leader
3. If ESRCH (not group leader): fall back to child.kill()
4. Always call child.wait() to reap zombie
5. Never panic in Drop

## Required Tests

Unit tests:
1. drop_kills_running_process — spawn sleep 60 via spawn(), drop, verify dead
2. drop_kills_descendants — spawn shell that forks grandchildren, drop, verify all dead
3. drop_during_panic_cleans_up — catch_unwind panic, verify process dead after unwind
4. wait_with_output_consumes_child — fast process, call wait_with_output, verify no kill
5. already_exited_no_error — spawn true, sleep, drop — no error
6. no_zombie_after_drop — fast process exits, drop guard, verify no zombie (waitpid WNOHANG returns ECHILD)
7. fallback_kills_non_group_leader — ChildGuard::new() without process_group(0), verify individual kill works
8. concurrent_guards_all_cleanup — 10 guards, drop all, verify all dead

Property test:
- no_process_leak(n in 1..=12) — spawn N guards, drop, verify all PIDs dead
close_reason: Added ChildGuard RAII wrapper with spawn() and new() constructors, kill-on-drop semantics, wait_with_output() consumption, and 8 unit tests + 1 property test

title: Add ProcessSemaphore to shutdown crate and consolidate duplicates
description:
Move the ProcessSemaphore (counting semaphore for throttling concurrent process spawns) into crates/shutdown/src/lib.rs. Currently copy-pasted identically in 3 files:
- springfield/tests/integration.rs (as SgfSemaphore, ~45 lines)
- pensa/tests/integration.rs (as ProcessSemaphore, ~45 lines)
- claude-wrapper/tests/integration.rs (as ProcessSemaphore, ~45 lines)

## API

- ProcessSemaphore::new(max: usize)
- ProcessSemaphore::from_env(var: &str, default: usize) — read max from env var, fall back to default
- sem.acquire() -> ProcessSemaphoreGuard — blocks until permit available, RAII release on drop

## Migration

Each test file keeps its own static + wrapper (one-liners):
  static PERMITS: LazyLock<ProcessSemaphore> = LazyLock::new(|| ProcessSemaphore::from_env("SGF_TEST_MAX_CONCURRENT", 8));
  fn run_sgf(cmd: &mut Command) -> Output { let _p = PERMITS.acquire(); cmd.output().expect("failed") }

Remove ~45 lines of duplicated struct/impl from each test file.

## Required Tests

Unit tests:
1. acquire_up_to_max — acquire N=max permits, all succeed
2. acquire_blocks_at_max — at max, new acquire blocks until release
3. guard_releases_on_drop — acquire, drop, re-acquire succeeds
4. from_env_reads_variable — set env var, verify max respected
5. from_env_uses_default — no env var, verify default used

Property test:
- active_permits_never_exceed_max(max in 2..=8, n_threads in 2..=16) — spawn threads, assert concurrent active count <= max
close_reason: ProcessSemaphore consolidated into shutdown crate with full test coverage; 3 test files deduplicated

title: Add SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID env var to skip setsid() in tests
description:
setsid() in AFK mode puts child processes in their own session, making them immune to parent process group kills. In production this is correct (terminal detachment). In tests it causes process leaks because killing the parent does not propagate to setsid-isolated children.

## Changes

At all 4 setsid call sites, gate on SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID:

1. crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs:340-346 (AFK ralph spawn)
2. crates/springfield/src/cursus/runner.rs:234-239 (cursus ralph spawn)
3. crates/ralph/src/main.rs:537-540 (setsid_hook definition)
4. crates/ralph/src/main.rs:574-579 (AFK agent/cl spawn)

Pattern at each site:
  if afk && std::env::var("SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID").is_err() {
      unsafe { cmd.pre_exec(|| { libc::setsid(); Ok(()) }); }
  }

## Test Infrastructure

- sgf_cmd() in springfield/tests/integration.rs: add .env("SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID", "1")
- ralph_cmd() in ralph/tests/integration.rs: add .env("SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID", "1")

## Required Tests

1. With SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID=1: spawned child is in parent's process group (getpgid(child) == getpgid(parent))
2. Without env var: child IS in its own session (existing behavior preserved)
close_reason: Implemented SGF_TEST_NO_SETSID gating at all 4 setsid call sites and both test helpers. Added 2 unit tests.

ralph/tests/integration.rs has 62 tests with 58 unguarded .output() calls and no ProcessSemaphore. This is the only crate with integration tests that spawn subprocesses without concurrency throttling (springfield, pensa, and claude-wrapper all have it).

## Changes

2. Add static PERMITS + run_ralph() wrapper function (using ProcessSemaphore from shutdown crate)
3. Wrap all ralph_cmd().output() calls through run_ralph()
4. Also wrap setup_test_dir() git Command::output() calls through the semaphore

title: AFK tests run_afk_passes_log_file and run_no_push_passes_auto_push_false fail with exit 130 due to stdin EOF
description: In crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs, tests run_afk_passes_log_file (~line 838) and run_no_push_passes_auto_push_false (~line 874) fail consistently with exit code 130 instead of 0. The ShutdownController detects stdin EOF as double Ctrl-D because the test process stdin is a pipe that returns EOF repeatedly, causing rapid eof_count increments. The controller prints 'Press Ctrl-D again to exit' and exits with code 130 (interrupted). These tests need SGF_MONITOR_STDIN=0 set in their environment, or the tests need to disable stdin monitoring.

title: Stdin monitoring thread steals input from interactive cursus iters
description: When running a multi-iter cursus with mixed AFK/interactive stages (e.g. sgf precatur), the ShutdownController is created once for the entire loop with monitor_stdin=true if any iter is AFK. The stdin monitoring thread holds a blocking read() on fd 0 for the entire cursus lifetime, consuming keystrokes meant for interactive cl sessions. Users cannot type into the claude window during interactive stages.
close_reason: fixed

title: Move ShutdownController creation inside per-iter loop body
description: The ShutdownController is created once for the entire cursus loop. Its is_afk check uses .any() across all remaining iters, enabling monitor_stdin for the whole loop even when only some iters are AFK. Fix: move controller creation inside the loop so monitor_stdin is based on the current iter's effective mode, and the controller is dropped between iters.
close_reason: Moved ShutdownController creation inside the per-iter loop in run_cursus_loop so monitor_stdin is based on the current iter's effective mode

title: Replace blocking stdin read with poll-based loop in ShutdownController
description: The stdin monitoring thread uses a blocking read() that cannot be interrupted by the stop flag. When the controller is dropped, the thread lingers and continues consuming stdin. Fix: use libc::poll() with a 200ms timeout so the thread checks stop between reads, store the JoinHandle, and join on Drop to guarantee the thread exits before the next iter.
close_reason: Replaced blocking read with libc::poll 200ms timeout, stored JoinHandle, join on Drop

title: Implement terminal settings preservation (tcgetattr/tcsetattr) in sgf

This ensures the terminal is always in a known-good state for signal handling between iterations and after the agent run. If stdin is not a terminal, tcgetattr fails and no save/restore occurs (expected and harmless). Currently not implemented in orchestrate.rs or cursus/runner.rs.
close_reason: Added tcgetattr/tcsetattr terminal preservation to orchestrate.rs for both run_interactive_claude and run_ralph paths. Made save/restore functions pub in iter_runner.

title: Add context_producers field to RunMetadata struct
description: Spec requires context_producers: HashMap<String, String> in RunMetadata (meta.json) to track which iter last wrote each produces key. Currently missing from the struct in state.rs. Must be initialized empty, updated in the post-iter evaluation when check_produces succeeds, and serialized/deserialized with serde.
close_reason: Added context_producers HashMap to RunMetadata struct, initialized empty, updated on successful produces check, with serde(default) for backward compat. 2 new tests.

title: Make SGF_RUN_CONTEXT return relative path from repo root
description: Spec requires SGF_RUN_CONTEXT=.sgf/run/<run-id>/context/ as a relative path from repo root. Current context_env_var() in context.rs returns an absolute path via dir.to_string_lossy(). Fix to return the relative .sgf/run/<run-id>/context/ path instead. Update the unit test assertion accordingly.
close_reason: context_env_var() now returns relative path .sgf/run/<run-id>/context/ per spec. Removed unused root param from context_env_var and invoke_cl.

title: Use runtime context_producers for resolve_consumes headers
description: Spec says the iter name in context headers reflects whichever iter LAST WROTE the file at runtime — not the static TOML declaration. Current build_key_to_iter_map() in context.rs builds from TOML definitions, so if draft and revise both produce draft-presentation, the header always shows revise even if the pipeline stalled before revise ran. Fix: accept RunMetadata.context_producers as input to resolve_consumes and prefer it over the static TOML map. Depends on the context_producers field being added to RunMetadata.
close_reason: resolve_consumes now accepts runtime context_producers and prefers them over static TOML definitions for iter name headers

title: Update stall banner to match spec box-drawing format
description: Spec requires stall banner with box-drawing characters showing Cursus name, Iter name, Reason, and resume command in a bordered box. Current print_stall_banner() in runner.rs uses style::print_warning_detail() which produces a simpler format. Update to match the spec format using existing style.rs box-drawing utilities.
close_reason: Updated print_stall_banner to use render_box_styled with box-drawing characters matching the cursus spec format. Added 3 unit tests.

title: Show accumulated context in resume action prompt
description: Spec says resume should present what context was accumulated alongside iter stall info. Current prompt_resume_action() in runner.rs shows iter name and completed count but does not list accumulated context keys. Add display of context_producers keys from RunMetadata.
close_reason: Added context_producers display to prompt_resume_action via format_context_line helper. Keys are sorted alphabetically and shown as a comma-separated list.

title: Add daemon env var stripping to sgf_cmd test helper
description: Spec section 5 (Enhanced sgf_cmd Helper) requires sgf_cmd() to call .env_remove() for PN_DAEMON, PN_DAEMON_HOST, FM_DAEMON, and FM_DAEMON_HOST to prevent daemon discovery leaks between tests. Current sgf_cmd() in integration.rs does not strip these vars. Add the four env_remove calls.
close_reason: Added env_remove calls for PN_DAEMON, PN_DAEMON_HOST, FM_DAEMON, FM_DAEMON_HOST to sgf_cmd test helper per test-harness spec §5

title: ProcessSemaphore::new(0) does not panic as spec requires
description: Spec says ProcessSemaphore::new() panics if max is 0 — at least one permit must be available. Current implementation in lib.rs:343 silently accepts max=0, which would cause all acquire() calls to deadlock. Add an assert\!(max > 0) or panic with descriptive message.
close_reason: Added assert\!(max > 0) panic in ProcessSemaphore::new() with three #[should_panic] tests covering new(0), from_env with env=0, and from_env with default=0.

title: Process exhaustion under concurrent cl usage due to kill_process_group checking leader PID not group, and build loop not resilient to spawn failures
description: When multiple cl instances run concurrently (even 2-3), process table fills up over time. Two root causes: (1) kill_process_group() checks if leader PID is dead rather than checking the process group, so it returns success prematurely while children survive. (2) The cursus build loop treats cargo test spawn failures as test failures instead of retrying with backoff.
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix kill_process_group to check group liveness instead of leader PID
description: Line 312 in shutdown/lib.rs uses kill(pid, 0) (positive pid — checks leader only). Should use kill(neg_pid, 0) to check the entire process group. When the leader exits but children remain, the function returns true prematurely and the SIGKILL fallback never fires.
close_reason: Fixed: kill_process_group now checks group liveness (neg_pid) not just leader PID

title: Add spawn failure retry with backoff to cursus build loop
description: When cargo test fails due to resource exhaustion (spawn failures), the cursus runner treats it as a test failure. Instead, detect resource-pressure exits and retry the iteration with backoff, giving the system time to reclaim processes.
close_reason: Added spawn_with_retry with EAGAIN detection and exponential backoff to invoke_ralph and invoke_cl

title: ralph signal tests (single_ctrl_c_continues_after_timeout, afk_single_ctrlc_resets_after_timeout) flaky — child process timeouts after 30s
description: Both tests intermittently fail with 'child process did not exit within 30s' despite wait_with_output_timeout(30s). The tests send a single SIGINT then expect the process to eventually exit, but the child hangs. Observed during full workspace test run.
close_reason: Fixed race condition: moved SGF_READY_FILE write after ShutdownController creation in run_cursus_loop. Added SGF_MONITOR_STDIN=0 to signal tests.

title: Test daemon processes (pn, fm) orphaned after test completion, causing process accumulation
description: Test fixtures (TestDaemon, DualDaemon, PensaOnlyDaemon) spawn daemon processes in background threads but have no Drop implementation to shut them down. Daemons only respond to SIGTERM/SIGINT which they never receive from tests, so they persist as orphans (reparented to PID 1) after tests complete. Under repeated cargo test runs, this accumulates hundreds of zombie daemon processes consuming system resources. Fixed by: (1) adding POST /shutdown endpoint to both forma and pensa daemons using tokio::sync::Notify, (2) adding Drop impls to all three test fixture structs that hit /shutdown on teardown.
close_reason: Fixed by adding POST /shutdown endpoint to forma and pensa daemons and Drop impls to all test fixtures

title: run_cursus_context_passing unit test fails — mock script double-slash in context path and missing --prompt-file assertion
description: The test cursus::runner::tests::run_cursus_context_passing fails with two issues: (1) mock script gets double-slash in context path (.sgf/run/<id>/context//output-summary.md), and (2) assertion expects --prompt-file in verify iter args but it is not present. Pre-existing failure, not caused by context_producers changes.
close_reason: Test passes — already fixed by pn-c67c6bd7

title: Fix: cli_client.rs TestEnv leaks orphaned daemon processes (missing Drop impl)
description: cli_client.rs TestEnv struct spawns a forma daemon thread but has no Drop implementation, causing every test to leak an orphaned daemon process. The integration.rs TestDaemon was fixed in commit 5b85e85 with a Drop impl calling /shutdown, but cli_client.rs was overlooked. Fix: add port and http fields to TestEnv, implement Drop to POST /shutdown.
close_reason: Fixed: added port and http fields to TestEnv struct, implemented Drop to POST /shutdown on cleanup. Matches pattern from integration.rs TestDaemon (commit 5b85e85).

title: Fix: pn/fm daemons orphaned when project directory is deleted (add watchdog to shutdown_signal)
description: ensure_daemon() in pensa/src/main.rs and forma/src/main.rs spawns daemon subprocesses via Command::new().spawn() that are intentionally detached. When spawned into temp directories (e.g. during tests), the daemon persists after the temp dir is deleted, orphaning the process. Fix: add a project-dir watchdog to daemon shutdown_signal that checks every 5s if the project directory still exists; if not, the daemon exits gracefully.
close_reason: Fixed: added project-dir watchdog to shutdown_signal in both pensa/src/daemon.rs and forma/src/daemon.rs. Daemons now check every 5s if their project directory exists and exit gracefully if it's been deleted. Also added project_dir field to FormaState.

title: run_cursus_context_passing test fails — verify iter does not receive --prompt-file arg

close_reason: Fixed SGF_RUN_CONTEXT to use absolute path and updated test to verify --append-system-prompt instead of --prompt-file

These failures pre-date the ralph->agent naming cleanup (pn-02ba5bb0) — verified by stashing changes and running old test names. Likely a regression from a previous iteration.
close_reason: Fixed resolve_agent_command defaults (ralph→cl) and updated 23 integration test assertions to match iter_runner architecture

title: sgf init warns about missing MEMENTO.md/BACKPRESSURE.md even when available in ~/.sgf

close_reason: Removed false-alarm warnings from init.rs; resolve_context_files already handles ~/.sgf/ fallback

title: Simple prompt mode inverts monitor_stdin flag, stealing keystrokes from child process
description: In run_simple_prompt(), monitor_stdin was set to \!afk && is_terminal() instead of afk && is_terminal(). This caused the shutdown controller stdin monitor thread to compete with the inherited-stdin child Claude process for input bytes, resulting in intermittent keystroke loss when typing into sgf <prompt.md> in interactive mode.
close_reason: Fixed inverted monitor_stdin flag in run_simple_prompt and added regression tests

title: run_afk() hangs indefinitely when Claude Code does not exit after emitting result event
description: The run_afk() function in iter_runner waits forever for the agent process stdout to close. When Claude Code emits its terminal result event but hangs during its own shutdown, the iteration loop stalls indefinitely. Confirmed in incarnatur-20260328T100256.log: token usage line printed but 'Iteration 11 complete' never appeared.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add post-result timeout to run_afk() to kill hung agent processes
description: After receiving a result event (Usage or Result variant), start a 30s deadline. If the agent process has not exited by then, kill it via kill_process_group and proceed. Timeout is configurable via IterRunnerConfig.post_result_timeout.
close_reason: Implemented post_result_timeout in run_afk(). Added configurable deadline (default 30s) after result event. 3 tests added: hung process killed, clean exit unaffected, result-without-usage also triggers timeout.

title: fm and pn daemons bind to 0.0.0.0 instead of 127.0.0.1, exposing unauthenticated endpoints to all network interfaces
description: Both forma and pensa daemons bind to 0.0.0.0 (all network interfaces) instead of 127.0.0.1. Combined with zero authentication on all endpoints (including destructive ones like DELETE /specs, DELETE /issues, POST /doctor?fix=true, POST /shutdown), any process on the network can interact with the daemons. Should bind to localhost only.
close_reason: Changed bind address from 0.0.0.0 to 127.0.0.1 in both forma and pensa daemons

title: Remove dead code from config.rs — consolidate Mode enum into cursus::toml
description: config.rs is 99% dead code. The only used item is the Mode enum (imported by orchestrate.rs), which is an exact duplicate of cursus::toml::Mode. Tasks: (1) Update orchestrate.rs to import Mode from crate::cursus::toml::Mode instead of crate::config::Mode. (2) Delete all dead functions from config.rs (parse, load, validate, resolve_alias, CommandConfig struct, etc.). (3) If config.rs becomes empty after removing dead code, delete the file and remove the pub mod config declaration from lib.rs. (4) Verify cargo build --workspace and cargo clippy --workspace pass.
close_reason: Deleted config.rs, updated orchestrate.rs to import Mode from cursus::toml, removed pub mod config from lib.rs. Build, clippy, and all tests pass.

(3) Architecture diagram lists config.rs as config.toml parsing. (4) Prompt delivery section mentions loading config.toml. Update the README to document the cursus-based architecture: cursus TOML pipeline definitions in .sgf/cursus/, command resolution via cursus module, iter execution, context passing, and stall recovery. Reference the main README.md for detailed cursus documentation where appropriate to avoid duplication.

title: fm export: remove stale .md files from .forma/specs/ for deleted specs
description: The spec says fm export should remove any .md files from .forma/specs/ whose filename stem does not match an active spec in the database. Currently export only writes new files but never cleans up orphaned markdown from deleted specs. Add stale file cleanup after writing spec markdown in db.rs export_jsonl(). Include an integration test: create spec, export, delete spec, export again, verify .md file is removed.

close_reason: Fixed README.md table column header from 'Src' to 'Code' in db.rs export_jsonl(), updated unit test and integration test assertions.

title: fm daemon: implement 3-consecutive-failure watchdog logic
description: The spec says the project directory watchdog should require 3 consecutive check failures (15 seconds total at 5s intervals) before shutting down, and a single successful check resets the counter. Currently daemon.rs shuts down immediately on the first directory-missing check. Add a failure counter that tracks consecutive failures and only triggers shutdown at 3. Include a test verifying the watchdog tolerates transient failures.
close_reason: Added failure counter requiring 3 consecutive directory-missing checks before shutdown. Single successful check resets counter. Added integration test verifying transient failure tolerance.

title: fm daemon status: print project directory alongside daemon URL
description: The spec says 'fm daemon status' should print the daemon URL and project directory if connected. Currently it only prints the URL. Read .forma/daemon.project and display the project directory in the status output.
close_reason: Implemented: fm daemon status now prints project directory from .forma/daemon.project alongside the daemon URL

title: Implement 3-consecutive-failure watchdog logic in pensa daemon
description: The project directory watchdog in daemon.rs triggers shutdown on the first failed exists() check. The spec requires 3 consecutive failures (15 seconds total) before triggering shutdown, with a single successful check resetting the failure counter to zero. This is needed to tolerate transient filesystem issues (NFS flakes, permission blips, momentary unmounts).
close_reason: Implemented 3-consecutive-failure watchdog logic with counter reset on directory reappearance. Added ignored integration test.

title: Add refs-in-show integration test

The DynamicArgs struct and parse_dynamic_args() function don't parse this flag. Add --skip-preflight to DynamicArgs and pass it through to CursusConfig.

title: Wire validate_aliases into cursus startup path
description: The spec says alias validation (unique aliases, no shadowing file names) is 'enforced at parse time (before any iter executes).' The validate_aliases function exists in cursus/toml.rs and has unit tests, but it is never called from main.rs or any production code path. validate() and validate_prompts() are both called at startup, but validate_aliases() is dead in the production path. Wire it into run_cursus_dispatch() after loading all cursus definitions from both local and global directories.

There is an integration test for the reject transition (run_cursus_reject_transition) but no corresponding test for the revise transition. title: fm delete never sends actor header to daemon
description: All mutations should log the actor in the audit trail. The --actor flag / FM_ACTOR env / git config fallback are documented as global. However, Client::delete_spec in client.rs does not accept an actor parameter and does not send the x-forma-actor header. Commands::Delete in main.rs does not pass actor to the client. The daemon handler does extract actor from headers, but since the client never sends it, delete events always have actor: null. Fix: add actor parameter to Client::delete_spec and thread it through from Commands::Delete.
close_reason: Added actor parameter to Client::delete_spec, send x-forma-actor header in delete request, pass actor from Commands::Delete. Updated cli_client test call site. Added actor_from_header_on_delete integration test.

title: fm count JSON output uses wrong field name and human output reads wrong key
description: Two related bugs in fm count: (1) The spec says fm count without flags returns {"count": N} but CountResult in db.rs uses field 'total', so output is {"total": N}. Fix: rename the field to 'count' or add #[serde(rename)]. (2) fm count --by-status human output in print_count (output.rs) reads g["key"] but StatusCount serializes with field name 'status', not 'key'. This causes grouped count human output to print '?: N' instead of 'draft: N'. Fix: read g["status"] instead of g["key"].
close_reason: Fixed CountResult field name from 'total' to 'count' and fixed human output key from 'key' to 'status'

title: Wire pre-launch lifecycle into cursus dispatch and simple prompt paths
description: The spec says before launching any loop, sgf runs pre-launch checks: (1) Recovery via pre_launch_recovery(), (2) Daemon startup via ensure_daemons(), (3) pn export. The logic exists in orchestrate.rs (dead code) and recovery.rs but is never called from the active code paths. Both run_cursus_dispatch() in main.rs and run_simple_prompt() go directly to the loop without any pre-launch steps. Wire up: (a) pre_launch_recovery() unconditionally, (b) ensure_daemons() gated by SGF_SKIP_PREFLIGHT env var (skips daemons but not recovery), (c) pn export before loop start. The --skip-preflight CLI flag (pn-ae36abf7) skips everything, while SGF_SKIP_PREFLIGHT skips only daemons.
close_reason: Wired pre_launch_recovery(), ensure_daemons(), and export_pensa() into both run_simple_prompt and run_cursus_dispatch. Added --skip-preflight CLI flag and SGF_SKIP_PREFLIGHT env var gating.

title: pn CLI uses cwd instead of walking up to find .pensa/ — causes data loss when agent cds into subdirectory
description: When pn commands run from a subdirectory (e.g. crates/lsr-app/), discover_port(), ensure_daemon(), and read_daemon_url() all use current_dir() directly instead of walking up to find the .pensa/ directory. This causes pn to spawn a new daemon with the wrong project root, connecting to a fresh empty SQLite database. All issues from the correct daemon appear lost. 25 occurrences found across localscribe build logs.
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix pn project root resolution — walk up directory tree to find .pensa/
description: Add find_project_root() to db.rs that walks up from cwd looking for .pensa/. Use it in client.rs (discover_port, read_daemon_url) and main.rs (ensure_daemon, is_remote_host) instead of raw current_dir().
close_reason: Added find_project_root() to db.rs, updated client.rs and main.rs to use it instead of raw current_dir()

title: find_project_root() fooled by rogue .pensa/ directories left by old binary cwd bug

close_reason: fixed

close_reason: Hardened find_project_root() with is_genuine_pensa_dir(), cleaned up rogue .pensa/ and 22k orphaned data dirs, reinstalled binary

title: Add programmatic turn-by-turn execution for interactive iters

On the resume, write the outer agent's stdin to cl's stdin. AFK iters run to completion internally with no turn events.
close_reason: Implemented programmatic turn-by-turn for interactive iters with WaitingForInput status, run_programmatic_turn function, and resume support. 8 new unit tests, all 69 runner tests passing.

title: Add programmatic resume with stdin action parsing for stalled runs
description: In programmatic mode, resume_cursus emits a stall event with available actions (retry, skip, abort). On next --resume invocation, parse first line of stdin as action. Execute the action: retry re-runs stalled iter, skip advances to next iter, abort marks run as interrupted. Unrecognized actions emit error event and exit 1.
close_reason: Implemented programmatic resume with stdin action parsing. Stalled runs in programmatic mode emit stall event, read action from stdin, execute retry/skip/abort. Added parse_resume_action helper with 5 unit tests and 3 scenario tests.

title: Implement unified resume dispatch in run_dynamic

This replaces the current Resume built-in command's dispatch logic. The dispatch should happen early in run_dynamic, before resolve_command.

title: Cursus resume restarts iter from scratch instead of resuming the conversation
description: When resuming an interrupted or stalled cursus run via --resume, the Retry option re-runs the iter with a fresh cl session instead of resuming the exact Claude conversation. The session_id is not persisted on interrupt/stall, and no Resume option exists to continue the interrupted conversation.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add conversation-level resume to cursus --resume
description: Save current_session_id on interrupt/stall, add Resume action to menu, thread resume session_id through run_cursus_loop → run_iter → cl --resume. Works for both human (terminal) and agent (programmatic) callers.
close_reason: Implemented conversation-level resume. Session_id persisted on interrupt/stall, Resume action added to menu for both terminal and programmatic modes, resume session_id threaded through to cl --resume.

title: Agents calling sgf c pass description as positional arg instead of piping via stdin
close_reason: fixed

title: Resume hint shown on completed runs
description: sgf shows 'To resume: sgf ... --resume ...' even when a run finishes in completed state, which is confusing since there is nothing to resume
close_reason: fixed

title: Suppress resume hint when run completes successfully
description: Gate CLI resume hint and programmatic resume_command on non-completed status. Make resume_command Optional in RunComplete event.
close_reason: Implemented: resume hint suppressed for completed runs in CLI and programmatic output

title: Resume hint not shown when Ctrl+C exits an sgf create instance
description: No distinction between Ctrl+C exit and successful completion — both suppress the resume hint since commit 05bb6ac
close_reason: fixed

title: Always show resume hint on run exit
description: Revert commit 05bb6ac which suppressed resume hint on completed runs. Resume hint should always be shown so users can easily resume regardless of how they exited.
close_reason: Reverted commit 05bb6ac via git revert

title: sgf resume subcommand missing — no way to list and pick from resumable sessions
close_reason: fixed

title: Add sgf resume subcommand with session picker
description: Add a Resume built-in to sgf CLI that lists resumable sessions (cursus + legacy) and lets the user pick one. Also supports sgf resume <run-id> as a shortcut.
close_reason: Implemented sgf resume subcommand with picker and direct-resume modes

title: Programmatic interactive cursus completes after one turn instead of waiting for input
description: When an interactive cursus is invoked programmatically (piped stdin, e.g. echo '...' | sgf c), the run immediately completes after a single turn because the turn-based waiting_for_input flow was gated on output_format_json (explicit --output-format json flag) rather than the programmatic flag. This meant piped callers could not resume the session to send follow-up input.
close_reason: fixed

title: Unify programmatic and output_format_json gates for interactive turn-based flow
description: Remove CursusConfig.output_format_json field; gate the interactive turn-based flow on config.programmatic instead. Fix run_programmatic_turn to respect auto_push from iter config and inherit stdin on initial turns. Update integration tests to match new semantics: non-TTY interactive cursuses enter turn-based flow with waiting_for_input.
close_reason: Implemented and all tests pass (978 tests, 0 failures)

title: Programmatic interactive turn returns empty content — piped stdin never reaches cl
description: After unifying the programmatic gate, echo '...' | sgf c enters the turn-based flow which uses run_programmatic (--print mode). In --print mode, cl takes the prompt from args and ignores stdin. The piped user message never reaches cl, resulting in empty turn content and sessions that cannot be resumed because cl received only the iter prompt file, not the user's request.
close_reason: fixed

title: Read piped stdin as user input for programmatic interactive turns
description: For initial programmatic interactive turns, read sgf stdin and pass as main prompt to cl. Move iter prompt file to --append-system-prompt (instructions context). Revert stdin inheritance in run_programmatic back to Stdio::null since user input is now passed via args.
close_reason: Implemented — piped stdin correctly flows to cl as user message

title: run_programmatic passes --verbose to cl, causing --output-format json to return an array instead of a single object — parser extracts empty result/session_id
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix programmatic mode JSON parsing and empty session_id guard
description: Remove --verbose from run_programmatic (not needed for single-blob JSON output), add array-aware parser fallback, guard against saving empty session_id in cursus metadata
close_reason: Fixed: removed --verbose from run_programmatic, added array-aware JSON parser, guarded against empty session_id

title: sgf kill command missing — no way to externally stop a running cursus and mark it resumable
close_reason: fixed

title: Add sgf kill <run-id> subcommand
description: Add a Kill subcommand that reads cursus metadata, kills the process group if alive, sets status to WaitingForInput, and removes the PID file.
close_reason: Implemented sgf kill with tests — 7 new tests covering not-found, already-terminal, dead PID, no PID, live PID kill, session_id preservation, and iter position preservation.

title: Interactive sessions had no transcript logging and resume failed for crashed sessions
close_reason: fixed

title: Add PTY tee for interactive transcript logging and fix resume for crashed sessions
description: PTY tee module for interactive mode, session_id written before spawn, crashed sessions in resume picker, resume fallback for expired sessions, programmatic mode log persistence
close_reason: Implemented PTY tee for interactive transcripts, fixed metadata timing (session_id before spawn), included crashed sessions in resume picker, added expired session fallback

title: Heredoc piped stdin to sgf c fails with 'Not logged in' because multiline content passed as CLI argument
close_reason: fixed

description: In run_programmatic_turn, when user_input is provided via piped stdin, write it to a context file (_stdin.md) and pass as @file reference to cl, instead of passing raw text as a CLI argument which fails with multiline heredoc content.
close_reason: Implemented: stdin content written to _stdin.md context file, passed as @file reference to cl

title: Log files contain raw ANSI escape codes from PTY and programmatic mode
close_reason: Fixed by stripping all ANSI/terminal escapes before writing to log files

title: Strip ANSI escapes from log files in PTY tee and programmatic mode
description: Enhance strip_ansi to handle all CSI/OSC/Fe sequences and carriage returns. Apply stripping in pty_tee.rs and run_programmatic before writing to log files.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive ANSI stripping in strip_ansi, applied to PTY tee and programmatic mode log writes

title: iter_runner passes --session-id alongside --resume, causing Claude Code CLI rejection
description: run_interactive and run_afk unconditionally pass --session-id before the resume check, so when resuming they emit both --session-id and --resume. Claude Code CLI rejects this combo without --fork-session. run_programmatic already handled this correctly by only passing --session-id in the non-resume branch.
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix --session-id/--resume flag collision in run_interactive and run_afk
description: Move --session-id into the else (non-resume) branch in run_interactive and run_afk, matching run_programmatic's existing correct pattern.
close_reason: Fixed by moving --session-id into non-resume branch in run_interactive and run_afk

title: Agents use heredoc to pipe content to sgf despite MEMENTO warning, causing empty stdin and confusing 'Not logged in' errors
close_reason: fixed

title: Restructure MEMENTO.md and add empty-stdin warning event
description: 1. Restructure MEMENTO.md: move behavioral rules to top of document (before pn/fm command references) for better agent attention. Prominently surface heredoc prohibition. 2. Add initial_input field to CursusConfig so stdin is read at the boundary (main.rs) instead of inside the runner. 3. Emit non-fatal error event when programmatic interactive mode receives empty stdin, making the failure diagnosable in NDJSON output.
close_reason: Restructured MEMENTO.md with rules at top, added CursusConfig.initial_input and empty-stdin warning event

title: Ctrl+C during interactive cursus sets status to completed instead of interrupted
description: When user Ctrl+C's out of an interactive sgf cursus session, the run status is saved as completed because: (1) raw mode suppresses SIGINT delivery to the parent, (2) the child exits with code 0, and (3) detect_outcome treats exit code 0 as complete. This prevents --resume from working afterward.
close_reason: Fixed by tracking ctrl_c_forwarded flag through PTY runner → iter_runner → cursus runner. Also fixed the same bug in orchestrate.rs resume paths.

title: Silent agent death in run_afk() undetectable
description: Between iter_start and the first turn event, there is total silence in AFK mode. The receive loop spins on 100ms RecvTimeoutError::Timeout forever when the inner session dies without closing stdout. No way to distinguish a working agent from a dead one. TeeWriter::writeln() used println\!() which could silently panic on broken pipe.
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix silent agent death with heartbeats, inactivity timeout, and no-output detection
description: Implemented three fixes for silent agent death in run_afk(): (1) Heartbeat events emitted every 60s via tee.writeln() while child is alive and no result received yet. Env override: SGF_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECS. (2) Non-zero exit on inner session failure — tracks got_any_output bool; if child exits with no output, emits error event and overrides exit code to 1. (3) Inactivity timeout fallback at 30 minutes (SGF_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_SECS). Heartbeats do NOT reset last_activity_at. Also fixed TeeWriter to use explicit stdout lock with error logging instead of println\!() macro, and added process liveness detection via try_wait() to catch dead processes even when stdout pipe is held open by orphaned children.
close_reason: fixed

title: Pipe drain on dead child loses final output
description: When the child process exits but the stdout pipe is still open (held by reader thread buffer), the previous implementation broke out of the read loop immediately, potentially losing result/usage events that were already buffered in the channel. Additionally, reader_handle.join() could be called after it was already moved in the shutdown path.
close_reason: fixed

title: Fix pipe drain on dead child to preserve final output
description: Drain remaining buffered output from reader thread before breaking out of the read loop when child exits. Wrap reader_handle in Option to prevent double-join. Update springfield spec to reflect the new drain behavior.

title: Silent agent death — run_afk() exits 0 with no NDJSON output when inner session dies
description: Between iter_start and first turn event, total silence with no way to distinguish working from dead. Receive loop spins on RecvTimeoutError::Timeout forever when inner session dies without closing stdout. TeeWriter panics on broken pipe causing NDJSON loss even when inner session completes successfully.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add heartbeat events, liveness detection, and TeeWriter robustness to run_afk()
description: Implemented: (1) heartbeat events every 60s via try_wait() liveness check, (2) non-zero exit on inner session failure with no output, (3) 30-min inactivity timeout as fallback, (4) TeeWriter robustness replacing println\!() with explicit stdout lock + writeln\!() with error logging.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 5bf167f
